testCaseName: E2E: Full Hiring Lifecycle with Multiple Jobs, Candidates and Cascading Selections
testCaseDescription: End-to-end hiring lifecycle: Admin/Recruiter/Candidate registration, multiple job creations, multi-job applications, duplicate prevention, multi-stage status updates, cascading selection (selected=SELECTED, others=REJECTED, job=deactivated), cross-job isolation, multi-recruiter independence, post-closure blocking, job deletion and admin user deletion with auth verification

steps.step: Register Admin user: POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register
Register Recruiter 1 (Jaydeep): POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register
Register Recruiter 2 (Smitha): POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register
Register Candidate 1 (Manik), Candidate 2 (Prajwal), Candidate 3 (Omkar): POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register (x3)
Admin logs in and views all registered users: GET http://localhost:5000/api/admin/users
Recruiter 1 (Jaydeep) logs in and creates Job 1 (Backend Engineer): POST http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
steps.requestBody:
{
  "title": "Backend Engineer",
  "description": "Java Spring Boot developer",
  "location": "Bangalore, India",
  "company": "CloudNine Technologies",
  "salary": "12,00,000 - 18,00,000",
  "type": "FULL_TIME"
}

steps.step: Recruiter 1 creates Job 2 (Frontend Developer): POST http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
Recruiter 2 (Smitha) logs in and creates Job 3 (Data Analyst): POST http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
steps.requestBody:
{
  "title": "Data Analyst",
  "description": "Python and SQL data analyst",
  "location": "Chennai, India",
  "company": "InfoSystems Ltd",
  "salary": "8,00,000 - 12,00,000",
  "type": "FULL_TIME"
}

steps.step: Verify all 3 jobs are active and visible in listings: GET http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
Candidate 1 (Manik) applies to Job 1 and Job 2 simultaneously: POST http://localhost:5000/api/applications (x2)
Candidate 1 (Manik) attempts duplicate apply to Job 1 — expects 409 Conflict: POST http://localhost:5000/api/applications
Candidate 2 (Prajwal) applies to Job 1 and Job 3; Candidate 3 (Omkar) applies to Job 2 and Job 3: POST http://localhost:5000/api/applications (x4)
Recruiter 1 views applications for Job 1 only (isolation check): GET http://localhost:5000/api/applications/job/1
Recruiter 1 updates Manik's Job 1 application to INTERVIEW_SCHEDULED, Prajwal's to ON_HOLD: PUT http://localhost:5000/api/applications/{id}/status
Candidate 1 (Manik) verifies dashboard shows INTERVIEW_SCHEDULED for Job 1 and PENDING for Job 2 (real-time + cross-job isolation): GET http://localhost:5000/api/applications/my
Recruiter 1 selects Manik for Job 1 — triggers auto-rejection of Prajwal and job deactivation: PUT http://localhost:5000/api/applications/1/status?status=SELECTED
Verify Prajwal auto-REJECTED for Job 1 and Job 1 deactivated; Manik's Job 2 still PENDING (cross-job isolation): GET http://localhost:5000/api/applications/job/1 + GET http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
Recruiter 2 (Smitha) independently selects Prajwal for Job 3 — cascading rejection of Omkar for Job 3, Job 3 deactivated: PUT http://localhost:5000/api/applications/4/status?status=SELECTED
New candidate attempts to apply for closed Job 1 — expects 400 error: POST http://localhost:5000/api/applications
Recruiter 1 deletes Job 2 (Frontend Developer) — job removed from listings: DELETE http://localhost:5000/api/jobs/2
Verify Job 2 no longer in listings and no active jobs remain: GET http://localhost:5000/api/jobs
Admin deletes Candidate 3 (Omkar): DELETE http://localhost:5000/api/admin/users/6
Verify deleted Candidate 3 (Omkar) can no longer authenticate: POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/login
